ingredients.item: 1½ pounds (650 g) bone-in veal (see Notes), cut into 2-inch (5 cm) pieces
ingredients.original: 1½ pounds (650 g) bone-in veal (see Notes), cut into 2-inch (5 cm) pieces

ingredients.item: 2 teaspoons kosher salt, divided, plus more if needed
ingredients.original: 2 teaspoons kosher salt, divided, plus more if needed

ingredients.item: 28 ounces (800 g) frozen small okra (see Notes)
ingredients.original: 28 ounces (800 g) frozen small okra (see Notes)

ingredients.item: 1 Season the veal pieces with ½ teaspoon of the salt and the pepper.
ingredients.original: 1 Season the veal pieces with ½ teaspoon of the salt and the pepper.

ingredients.item: 2 In a large pot over medium heat, add the oil, onion, and ½ teaspoon of the salt and cook, stirring often, for 5 to 6 minutes, until the onion is softened and translucent. Add the veal and sear for 5 to 6 minutes, flipping until all sides are lightly browned. Stir in the tomato paste and continue to cook for another few minutes. Add the okra, garlic, pomegranate molasses, remaining 1 teaspoon salt, and about 6 cups (1.4 L) of water to the pot and mix everything well. Bring to a rolling boil over medium heat, then boil for about 30 minutes with the pot half-covered with the lid.
ingredients.original: 2 In a large pot over medium heat, add the oil, onion, and ½ teaspoon of the salt and cook, stirring often, for 5 to 6 minutes, until the onion is softened and translucent. Add the veal and sear for 5 to 6 minutes, flipping until all sides are lightly browned. Stir in the tomato paste and continue to cook for another few minutes. Add the okra, garlic, pomegranate molasses, remaining 1 teaspoon salt, and about 6 cups (1.4 L) of water to the pot and mix everything well. Bring to a rolling boil over medium heat, then boil for about 30 minutes with the pot half-covered with the lid.

ingredients.item: 3 After 30 minutes, check the tenderness of the okra and the meat and taste and adjust the salt and pepper if necessary. If the okra and meat are both still slightly firm, add more water (2 to 3 cups, or 480 to 720 ml) and continue boiling for another 30 minutes; if they are both tender, turn off the heat and serve.
ingredients.original: 3 After 30 minutes, check the tenderness of the okra and the meat and taste and adjust the salt and pepper if necessary. If the okra and meat are both still slightly firm, add more water (2 to 3 cups, or 480 to 720 ml) and continue boiling for another 30 minutes; if they are both tender, turn off the heat and serve.
